Title: adjust_timezone
Description:
returns its first argument with unchanged GMT value but new timezone offset, as it is specified by the
    second argument

The function returns its first argument with unchanged GMT value but new timezone offset, as it is specified by the
    second argument. If the first argument is timezoneless and third argument is missing or zero then error 22023 is signaled.
    If the first argument is timezoneless and third argument is nonzero then no error is signaled and the argument is handled
    like it is a GMT value.
Examples:
SQL> SELECT adjust_timezone(cast('2014-12-31 15:00-12:00' as datetime),0,1) ;
DATETIME
2015-01-01 03:00:00
No. of rows in result: 1
